text: We further examined whether the loss of OPALIN affects mature OLs and OPCs. Immunostaining revealed that CC1+ cells and Olig2+ cells were decreased in the CC of Opalin cKO mice compared with control mice ( Fig. 3 F and G ). Furthermore, among Olig2+ cells, the percentage of CC1+ mature OLs was reduced in the CC of Opalin cKO mice ( Fig. 3 H ). However, the average number of PDGFRα+ OPCs did not differ significantly between the control and Opalin cKO mice ( Fig. 3 I and J ). Thus, the deletion of OPALIN does not affect the OPC population but reduces the mature OL population.
summary:
Non–OLIG2-centered.

In Opalin conditional knockout mice, Olig2+ cells were decreased in the corpus callosum compared with control mice. Among Olig2+ cells, the percentage of CC1+ mature oligodendrocytes was reduced in the corpus callosum of Opalin cKO mice.

text: Next, we conducted Nissl staining to examine white matter integrity ( Fig. 5 C ). The Opalin icKO mice exhibited reduced CC thickness ( Fig. 5 D ). Furthermore, the number of MBP+, MAG+, and PLP1+ fibers in the CC was lower in the Opalin icKO mice than in the littermate controls ( Fig. 5 E – G ). The number of CC1+ OLs and the ratio of CC1+/Olig2+ cells to Olig2+ cells were significantly decreased in the Opalin icKO mice ( Fig. 5 H – J ). However, the number of PDGFRα+ OPCs in the CC or cortex did not change in the Opalin icKO mice ( Fig. 5 K ). Moreover, the ratio of BrdU+/CC1+ cells to CC1+ cells was decreased in the Opalin icKO mice ( Fig. 5 L ). Taken together, the above results confirm that ablation of OPALIN in OPCs leads to hypomyelination and deficient OL differentiation.
summary: In Opalin icKO mice the ratio of CC1+/Olig2+ cells to Olig2+ cells in the corpus callosum was significantly decreased.

text: To verify whether OL differentiation was affected by OPALIN deletion, we conducted BrdU birth-dating experiments. BrdU was injected intraperitoneally into mice at P7 once a day for three consecutive days, and CC1/BrdU costaining was subsequently performed on brain sections prepared from the mice at P14 ( Fig. 4 F ). We found that the ratio of BrdU+/CC1+ cells to CC1+ cells was significantly decreased in the Opalin cKO mice ( Fig. 4 G and H ), indicating that OL differentiation was impaired. We also conducted BrdU pulse-labeling experiments to investigate whether OPALIN deletion affected OPC proliferation. BrdU was injected into the mice at P7, and brain tissues were collected 1 h later. The ratio of BrdU+/PDGFRα+ cells to PDGFRα+ cells was not altered in the Opalin cKO mice ( Fig. 3 K ). The ratio of Ki67+/Olig2+ cells to Olig2+ cells did not change in the Opalin cKO mice ( Fig. 4 I and J ). Overall, these results indicate that OPALIN is critical for OL differentiation but not OPC proliferation.
